assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.29) when 1.28 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be auto-upgraded to 1.29 when 1.28 reaches EoS and mentions this cannot be prevented

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.28 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states Extended channel clusters get 'up to 24 months total support (instead of the standard 14 months)' and can stay on 1.28 beyond standard EoS date

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters face systematic node-level EoS enforcement — nodes on EoS versions are force-upgraded
assertions.reason: Response specifically explains that No channel clusters face 'Node-level enforcement' where 'Individual nodes on EoS versions are systematically force-upgraded'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed maintenance exclusion commands and explains they can control timing, though it doesn't specifically mention the 30-day limit or that it defers EoS force-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response strongly recommends proactive manual upgrades as Option 1 and provides detailed action plan with immediate, short-term, and execution phases

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking deprecated APIs using kubectl command but does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not mention 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions and their associated security/reliability risks
